claim: Approximately $230 million in precious metals — including 379,036 ounces of gold and ~29.94 million ounces of silver belonging to Scotia Mocatta (the bullion division of Bank of Nova Scotia) — was stored in a two-story vault beneath WTC 4. Beginning October 30, 2001, recovery crews extracted the bullion via a delivery tunnel running beneath WTC 5, removing a ten-wheel truck and crushed vehicles to reach the vault. The metals were transferred to a Brooklyn Naval Yard vault. The COMEX metals trading division of NYMEX held additional vaults at the WTC complex containing 3,800 gold bars (~12 tons, $100M+), an additional ~800,000 ounces of gold held for others (~$220M), and 102+ million ounces of silver (~$430M).

sources.label: CNN — “Precious metals buried under debris” (Sep 22, 2001)
sources.quote: Tons of gold and silver had been stored in underground vaults beneath 4 World Trade Center

sources.label: The Globe and Mail — “Gold, silver recovered at attack site”
Billings Gazette — “Gold recovered from WTC site”
Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: Bullion recovery is well-documented across contemporaneous business press. CNN article is the closest contemporaneous primary news source. The reel's framing of 'truckloads of gold' as criminal proceeds is fictional within the narrative; the recovery operation as a real event is documented. URLs not yet directly fetched in this pass.
